title: Earnings call transcript: Vir Biotech's Q1 2026 sees wider losses, stock dips
text: Vir Biotechnology reported significant losses in Q1 2026. The company posted an EPS of -$0.85, substantially missing the -$0.10 forecast, while revenue came in at negative $29,000 versus the projected $55.89 million. Q1 2026 net loss: $125.7 million (up 3.9% year-over-year from $121 million). R&D expenses: $108.9 million (down 8.2% YoY). SG&A expenses: $23.3 million (down 2.5% YoY). Following the earnings announcement, VIR stock declined 7.39% in after-hours trading, closing at $9.27. Despite this setback, the stock has gained 75% over the past year and 63% over six months. The company closed its landmark $1.7 billion collaboration with Astellas on April 15, 2026, for VIR-5500 (a PSMA-targeted T-cell engager). CEO Marianne De Backer noted that joint teams are operational and partnering closely on a shared clinical development plan. With recent collaborations and a $172.5 million equity offering, the company ended Q1 with $809.3 million in cash. Including post-quarter Astellas proceeds, cash runway extends into late 2028. VIR-5500 Phase I data demonstrated favorable safety with mostly grade 1 cytokine release syndrome and deep PSA/RECIST responses in heavily pretreated patients. Hepatitis Delta: Phase II SOLSTICE data showed 88% of patients achieved undetectable HDV RNA with combination therapy (tobevibart + elebsiran) versus 46% on antibody monotherapy alone.
